verification_method: bun -e one-liners from packages/opencode/ + source inspection of 27 actual built-in tool files
critical_context: The plan describes work (27 new tool files, 6 built-in merges) that was ALREADY COMMITTED in a1414d8c8. All 27 tools already exist as Effect Tool.define modules. This evaluation treats the plan's proposed changes as its testability claims.

assessment_summary.score_rationale: Below 3.0 means most changes cannot be verified with ≤10-line bun -e scripts. File I/O patterns are independently testable, but every tool's actual execution (Effect runtime, AppFileSystem, InstanceState, Schema validation) requires full-layer provisioning that exceeds 10 lines.
assessment_summary.blocking_finding: The plan has NO per-tool test strategy — it identifies 'no tests exist' as a risk (severity: medium) but does not prescribe test approaches per tool. Tests are deferred to 'P2 work' with no strategy.
assessment_summary.accuracy_finding: Plan claims '27 new tools to create' but all already exist. Claims '6 existing built-ins to merge' but all 6 already exist and are registered. Claims 'no tests exist' — true for the 27 new tools, but 27 other tool test files DO exist in test/tool/, demonstrating the infrastructure exists but wasn't applied to these tools.

file_based_tools.evidence: FILE_IO_TEST: PASS (7 lines), JSONL_APPEND_TEST: PASS (8 lines) — Bun.file().write(), Bun.file().json(), file append all work standalone.
file_based_tools.why_full_execution_fails: Every tool wraps logic in Effect.gen(function* () { const fs = yield* AppFileSystem.Service; const instance = yield* InstanceState.context; ... }). Testing AppFileSystem alone outside the app runtime failed: 'undefined is not an object (evaluating layer.build)'. Each tool requires: Effect runtime, AppFileSystem layer, InstanceState layer, Schema decoder initialization, Tool.define init context.
file_based_tools.existing_test_model: failure-tools.test.ts (35 imports, full TestInstance + Layer.provide chain — 155 lines minimal). Even 'simple' tool tests require provisioning 20+ services (Agent, Provider, Session, Git, LSP, Bus, etc.).

duckdb_aware_dual_backend.evidence: JSONL_APPEND_TEST: PASS (8 lines), SHA256_DEDUP_TEST: PASS (8 lines for dedup logic). The JSONL write path is pure file I/O.
duckdb_aware_dual_backend.why_duckdb_fails: DuckDB subprocess spawn requires: path resolution, firewall bypass logic, pipe error handling, optional runtime flag. The duckdb-query.test.ts is 139 lines with fake client mock.
duckdb_aware_dual_backend.dual_path_verification_gap: The dual-backend pattern creates two code paths per tool. The plan does not describe how to test that both paths produce identical output. SHA-256 dedup (out-of-scope-finding) works in 8 lines standalone, but the cross-path consistency cannot be verified without both DuckDB and JSONL paths executing.

complex_effect_tools.evidence: Import extraction (filter lines starting with 'import') is testable in 5 lines. Symbol extraction via regex is testable in 8 lines. File read via Bun.file() is testable in 3 lines.
dependency_chain: read-source.ts: requires Git service (dirty detection, git blame, git log) — 358 lines total, 7 imports including Git
smart-edit.ts: requires edit metadata recording, existing file state, atomic replace — 153 lines, 6 imports
smart-batch.ts: requires atomic multi-file operations with rollback — multi-file state management
replace-symbol.ts: requires ast-grep or regex transformation with git state
read-lib.ts: requires node_modules path resolution outside workspace
read-artifact.ts: requires structured artifact format parsing with profile-based filtering

blocking_findings.finding: Plan claims '27 new tools to create' and '6 existing built-ins to merge' — all 33 tools ALREADY EXIST as built-in Effect Tool.define modules in packages/opencode/src/tool/, committed in a1414d8c8. The plan describes work already completed.
blocking_findings.evidence: smart_find on src/tool/ shows 55+ Tool.define() exports. All 27 listed files exist. All 6 'merge' files exist.
blocking_findings.repair_path: Revise plan to describe testing strategy for existing tools, not creation.

blocking_findings.finding: Plan has ZERO per-tool test strategy. Risk section lists 'no tests exist' at severity: medium but defers to 'P2 work' without describing per-tool test approaches, fixture patterns, or verification criteria.
blocking_findings.evidence: Full plan document search for 'test' yields only: risk description ('No tests exist'), 'P2 work', and a single reference to test file in execution step 14 ('bun test for existing tool tests'). No per-tool strategy.
blocking_findings.repair_path: Add a test strategy section with: (a) per-tool test approach, (b) dual-backend consistency verification plan, (c) JSONL fallback test pattern, (d) DuckDB integration test pattern, (e) Effect runtime test harness pattern.

blocking_findings.finding: Plan claims 'No tests exist' as a risk — this is technically true for the 27 tools, but 27 OTHER tool test files exist in test/tool/, proving the test infrastructure is mature. The plan doesn't reference existing patterns.
blocking_findings.evidence: test/tool/ has 27 test files: duckdb-query.test.ts (139 lines), failure-tools.test.ts (155 lines with 35 imports), etc. These demonstrate the TestInstance + Layer pattern.
blocking_findings.repair_path: Reference existing test patterns (TestInstance, testEffect helper, Layer provision chain) and describe how new tests follow the same pattern.

testability_score_breakdown.scale: 1-5 (5 = all changes verifiable in ≤10 line bun -e)

breakdown.explanation: JSON file read/write, JSONL append, directory creation are all testable in ≤10 lines with Bun.file(). These are the CORE OUTPUT patterns of 14 file-based tools.
SHA-256 hashing with crypto.subtle is testable in 8 lines. out-of-scope-finding's dedup logic can be verified independently.
DuckDB subprocess spawn cannot be tested in 10 lines. Requires firewall bypass, path resolution, error handling, dual output comparison.

Running a tool's Effect.gen execute() requires full layer provisioning. Even AppFileSystem.Default standalone failed ('layer.build is undefined'). Must use TestInstance pattern from existing tests (155+ lines).
Tools requiring Git service (read-source, smart-edit) cannot be tested without repository state, git blame, dirty detection. Not verifiable in 10 lines.
Schema.Struct validation IS testable with Schema.decodeUnknownSync in ~8 lines (verified: Schema rejects missing fields cleanly). But the tool integration with Tool.define init context is not.
The plan has NO per-tool test strategy. Score = 0 for this dimension.

effect_runtime_fail.verdict: FAIL ('layer.build is undefined' — requires full app runtime provisioning)
effect_runtime_fail.realistic_minimum: 155 lines + 35 imports (using existing TestInstance pattern from failure-tools.test.ts)

existing_test_infrastructure.example_complexity: failure-tools.test.ts: 155 lines, 35 imports from 20+ modules including Agent, Provider, Session, Git, LSP, Bus, Truncate, Storage, etc.
existing_test_infrastructure.key_finding: Even the 'simple' tool tests require full application layer provisioning. A 10-line bun -e script cannot substitute for the Effect runtime setup.

out_of_scope_findings.finding: Plan is stale — describes creation work already completed in a1414d8c8. Should be updated or archived and replaced with a test-strategy-targeted plan.
Existing 27 test files in test/tool/ demonstrate robust Effect-based test infrastructure but were not applied to the 27 new built-in tools. This suggests the tools were created without test coverage.

recommendations: Replace the stale plan with a test-strategy plan that covers each tool's test approach
Use the existing TestInstance pattern (from failure-tools.test.ts) as the test harness template
Implement JSONL fallback-only tests first (testable in 10 lines) as smoke tests
Add DuckDB dual-path consistency tests separately (not isolable in 10 lines)
Break complex tools (read-source, smart-edit) into testable utility functions (extractImports, extractSymbols, cleanContent) that ARE verifiable in 10 lines
